summary: Iter27 — Launch Orchestrator (wizard + backend job + polling). Validated: (1) BrandWizard renders all 5 steps with correct data-testid (wizard-step-identity/mood/typography/scope/launch). All inputs present (wizard-brand-name/tagline/mission/voice). Step 2 moods (éditorial/minimaliste/chaleureux/moderne) all present. Step 3: 4 font pairs. Step 4: scope-fill + scope-overwrite. Step 5: wizard-launch + wizard-back. Navigation via wizard-next advances steps correctly and récap screen shows filled data. (2) switch-to-wizard button present in advanced editor on a site that has an existing design (expected behavior — hasDesign=true). (3) Backend POST /api/sites/{id}/design/launch returns 200 with {ok:true, job_id, status:running}. (4) Concurrent guard: 2nd POST while running returns 409 with French message 'Une génération est déjà en cours pour ce site.' (5) GET /api/sites/{id}/design/launch-status returns latest job with correct structure {id, site_id, status, progress_pct, current_step, current_label, wizard, created_at, updated_at}. Status transitions running → completed verified (pre-existing job d8e6ce65 reached 100% 'Site généré !'). (6) Zombie reaper confirmed: backend restart during running job logged '[startup] reaped 1 zombie launch_jobs' and marked the job failed with error 'Serveur redémarré pendant la génération'. (7) Budget fail-soft confirmed in backend logs: logo step hit 402 budget exhausted → skipped without crashing job, continued to subsequent steps.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 as specified in review request. Payload {ok, job_id, status} is correct — just the status code. Not breaking the frontend (which checks for job_id only).

AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get' at launch.py:271 when the site is deleted mid-job (observed when fresh_site cleanup fired while orchestrator was running). Should guard `site2 = await db.sites.find_one(...)` with `if not site2: return`.
When an upstream site fetch returns 404 (site removed), these steps all log 'Site introuvable' — the orchestrator should abort early instead of trying every step.

action_items: (Minor) Return 201 instead of 200 on POST /api/sites/{id}/design/launch to align with the review request spec.

(Minor) Add `if not site2: return` guard at /app/backend/routes/launch.py line ~271 to prevent AttributeError crash when a site is deleted mid-job. Also consider aborting the whole orchestrator when an intermediate fetch returns 404.

(Info) The preview proxy returns 502 when the backend is fully busy running multiple concurrent launch jobs (observed with 2+ simultaneous jobs using Claude/Nano Banana with retries) — consider concurrency limits at cluster level OR a small semaphore in launch.py if more than N jobs running globally.
critical_code_review_comments: launch.py is 473 lines — borderline but acceptable for a single orchestrator; consider extracting the 9 steps into small async helpers (_step_logo, _step_homepage, ...) for readability.
launch.py line 271: `site2 = await db.sites.find_one(...)` result is not null-checked before `.get('design')` — causes AttributeError when site vanishes mid-job.
launch.py POST endpoint returns 200; FastAPI will default to 200 if status_code is not set. Should use @router.post(..., status_code=201).

BrandWizard.jsx uses hardcoded palettes per mood but `palettes[paletteIdx]` only has 1 entry per mood — paletteIdx is effectively always 0; dead state. Either remove paletteIdx or add multiple palettes per mood.

LaunchProgress.jsx polls every 2.5s — fine, but no abort on unmount / route change → possible state update on unmounted component warning. Add an AbortController or early return in the setInterval callback.
Regression check (from iter26 context): React warning `Invalid DOM property fetchpriority` still present in console — unrelated to this iteration.

success_rate.backend: 100% endpoint functionality (launch 200, status 200, concurrent 409, reaper OK, auth 401). 4/7 pytest tests passed; 3 pytest failures are network-timeout issues due to the backend being saturated by the concurrent launch job during test execution — functional behavior was separately verified via curl + logs.
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 18 wizard data-testids present, stepper navigates correctly, summary screen renders.

seed_data_creation: Launched one test job (6ab42069-4ed4-4241-8458-8dbfb910db88) on site 65964cb0 to validate 409 — killed by zombie reaper on backend restart. Also attempted a fresh TEST_LaunchWizard_Iter27 site via admin — cleanup executed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test site 65964cb0 still has valid design. Backend reaper confirmed working on startup. Concurrent guard 409 works. Budget fail-soft confirmed in logs. When running pytest on this testbed, the backend may be saturated if a launch job is running (preview proxy returns 502 to unrelated requests like /api/auth/login after ~60s). Prefer curl + logs over pytest for endpoints when a live job is in progress. The NoneType bug in _run_launch (line 271) is reproducible by deleting the site mid-job.
rca of the issue: No blocker bugs. The two minor backend issues (200 instead of 201, NoneType when site deleted) are corner-case code-quality improvements. Frontend is clean.
